tagline: Calculate bags, depth, and cost for blown-in fiberglass or cellulose

jsonLdWeb.name: Blown-In Insulation Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate bags, depth, and cost for blown-in fiberglass or cellulose insulation.

mainEntity.name: How many bags of blown-in insulation do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the area, desired R-value, and insulation type. For cellulose at R-38, you need about 1 bag per 27 sq ft. For fiberglass at R-38, about 1 bag per 22 sq ft. A 1,000 sq ft attic at R-38 needs roughly 37 bags of cellulose or 46 bags of fiberglass.

mainEntity.name: How deep should blown-in insulation be?

acceptedAnswer.text: For cellulose: R-38 requires about 10.5 inches, R-49 about 13.5 inches. For fiberglass: R-38 requires about 14 inches, R-49 about 18 inches. Cellulose settles about 20% over time, so it should be installed slightly thicker.

mainEntity.name: Fiberglass vs cellulose blown-in: which is better?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cellulose (recycled paper) is denser, resists airflow better, and has a higher R-value per inch (R-3.5 vs R-2.7). Fiberglass is lighter, does not absorb moisture, and won't settle as much. Cellulose is generally cheaper per R-value but heavier on ceiling joists.

mainEntity.name: Can I add blown-in insulation over existing insulation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Calculate the additional R-value needed by subtracting your existing R-value from the target. For example, if you have R-11 and want R-38, you need R-27 more. You can blow fiberglass over cellulose or vice versa, though matching types is ideal.

mainEntity.name: How much does blown-in insulation cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cellulose bags cost $10-$15 each (covering 27 sq ft at R-38). Fiberglass bags cost $25-$40 each (covering 22 sq ft at R-38). Many big-box stores offer free blower rental with purchase of 10+ bags. Professional installation adds $0.50-$1.50 per sq ft.

mainEntity.name: Does blown-in insulation settle over time?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cellulose settles about 15-20% over the first year. Fiberglass settles less, about 5-10%. Manufacturers account for settling in their coverage charts. Install at the 'initial installed' thickness listed on the bag to achieve the rated R-value after settling.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Blown-In Insulation

Determine the R-value gap (target minus existing), calculate the depth needed, then figure bags from area and coverage per bag.

Bags = Area (sq ft) ÷ Coverage per Bag (sq ft at target depth)
  • R-value needed: target R-value − existing R-value
  • Depth: R-value needed ÷ R-per-inch
  • Cellulose: R-3.5/inch, ~27 sq ft/bag at R-38
  • Fiberglass: R-2.7/inch, ~22 sq ft/bag at R-38
  • Settling: cellulose settles ~20%, fiberglass ~5-10%
  • Coverage per bag varies by depth — check the manufacturer's chart
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many bags do I need?

For cellulose at R-38: about 37 bags per 1,000 sq ft. For fiberglass at R-38: about 46 bags per 1,000 sq ft. Exact counts depend on the brand and bag size.

How deep should blown-in insulation be?

Cellulose at R-38: 10.9". Fiberglass at R-38: 14.1". Add depth for settling — cellulose settles about 20% in the first year.

Fiberglass vs cellulose?

Cellulose is denser, better at blocking air, and higher R-value per inch. Fiberglass is lighter, moisture-resistant, and settles less. Cellulose is usually cheaper.

Can I add over existing insulation?

Yes. Subtract existing R-value from target to find R-value needed. You can mix types, though matching is ideal.

How much does it cost?

Cellulose: $10-$15/bag. Fiberglass: $25-$40/bag. Many stores offer free blower rental with 10+ bags. Pro install adds $0.50-$1.50/sq ft.

Does it settle over time?

Cellulose settles 15-20%. Fiberglass settles 5-10%. Install at the manufacturer's listed initial thickness to account for settling.

sections.title: What Is the Blown-In Insulation Calculator?
sections.body: The Blown-In Insulation Calculator is a free online tool designed for contractors and DIY builders who need quick, accurate calculations in the construction and building space. By entering your area to insulate, insulation type, desired r-value, you get instant results including bags needed, depth required, r-value added. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting bags needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In construction and building, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by contractors and DIY builders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your area to insulate and need to find the right bags needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Area to Insulate (sq ft)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your area to insulate — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: insulation type, desired r-value, existing insulation r-value, price per bag. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your bags needed, depth required, r-value added. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your area to insulate before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.

faq.question: Is the Blown-In Insulation Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard construction and building formulas trusted by contractors and DIY builders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What area to insulate should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate area to insulate value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Blown-In Insulation Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

faq.question: What does bags needed mean in my results?
faq.answer: The bags needed value represents the primary output of the calculation based on your inputs. Use it as a baseline figure for your construction and building planning and decision-making.
